items.headline: Major elevator upgrades approved for Ridder Park office building
items.summary: A $1.1 million elevator modernization project at 1290 Ridder Park Dr will improve accessibility and safety at a significant commercial facility. This substantial infrastructure investment suggests the building is being repositioned or maintained as a major office hub.

items.headline: Lawsuit filed against city over Mary Avenue safety
items.summary: The Garden Gate Coalition is suing Cupertino over Mary Avenue safety concerns. The case is active in Santa Clara Superior Court and the city is in litigation defense.

items.headline: Commercial/industrial project underway on West San Carlos Street
items.summary: A $696,570 commercial or industrial project at 1300 W San Carlos St represents new business activity in San José's industrial corridor. The project could signal renewed investment in the area's manufacturing or logistics sector.

items.headline: City launches business amnesty program and retail incentives
items.summary: Cupertino is offering a business license amnesty program and economic development incentives targeting retail, small businesses, and startups. The city is also launching a summer shop-local campaign and expanding public-private partnerships for events.

items.headline: City seeking input on federal housing and community grants
items.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission reviewed applications for Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) funding on March 25, which supports affordable housing and community services. Residents can learn how federal funds are being allocated to address local housing needs.

items.headline: City updates zoning rules to boost retail shops and local businesses
items.summary: Palo Alto is amending its building and zoning codes to implement retail vitality policies from the Comprehensive Plan. This could affect where new retail can be built and how existing commercial areas develop.

items.headline: San Antonio Road area study examines land use and transportation changes
items.summary: The Council is holding a study session on alternatives for the San Antonio Road area, focusing on land use and transportation. This could signal future zoning or infrastructure changes in that corridor.

items.headline: City approves major power grid infrastructure across wastewater facility
items.summary: San José approved an easement agreement allowing LS Power Grid California to install electrical transmission infrastructure across the San José-Santa Clara Regional Wastewater Facility. This infrastructure project affects a major city facility and represents significant utility development in the region.
